subject: A Level Accounting
chapter_name: Correction of errors and suspense accounts

note_data.title: Correction of errors and suspense accounts
keyPoints: **Errors of omission** occur when a transaction is completely omitted from the books of account and do not affect trial balance totals
**Errors of commission** occur when correct amounts are posted to wrong accounts of the same class (e.g. wrong customer account) and do not affect trial balance totals
**Errors of principle** occur when transactions are posted to wrong class of account (e.g. revenue expenditure posted as capital expenditure) and do not affect trial balance totals
**Compensating errors** occur when two or more errors cancel each other out, leaving trial balance totals unaffected
**Errors of original entry** occur when wrong amounts are entered in both debit and credit entries from source document and do not affect trial balance totals
**Single-sided errors** affect only one side of double entry and cause trial balance to disagree, requiring a **suspense account**
**Suspense account** is a temporary account created to make trial balance agree when there are single-sided errors
When correcting errors through **journal entries**, use narration 'Correction of error' and ensure double entry is maintained
Errors affecting **profit calculation** require adjustment to retained earnings when corrected in following period
**Error correction** requires identification of accounts affected and reversal of incorrect entry followed by correct entry

definitions.definition: A temporary account created to record the difference in trial balance totals caused by single-sided errors until the errors are located and corrected
An error that occurs when a transaction is recorded in the wrong class of account, violating accounting principles
Two or more errors that cancel each other out, leaving the trial balance totals unaffected
An error where the correct amount is posted to the wrong account within the same class of accounts

howToAnswer.instruction: Name the type of error clearly without explanation
State the error type and explain why it occurred using 'because' or 'this is because'
Create proper journal entries with dates, account names, amounts, and narration
Show workings for suspense account balance or corrected profit figure

modelAnswer.question: A sale of Rs.15,000 to Ahmed was posted to Ali's account. Identify the type of error and prepare the journal entry to correct it.
modelAnswer.answer:
Type of error: Error of commission (1 mark)

Journal Entry:
Dr Ahmed's Account     Rs.15,000
Cr Ali's Account                Rs.15,000
Narration: Correction of error - sale wrongly posted to Ali instead of Ahmed
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correctly identified as error of commission

commonMistakes: Students confuse errors that affect trial balance with those that don't - only single-sided errors affect trial balance totals
Never say 'debit suspense account' without specifying the amount and reason - always show the calculation
Students often forget narration in journal entries - always include 'Correction of error' as narration
Students incorrectly identify error of commission as error of principle when posting to wrong customer accounts
Never prepare suspense account for errors of omission, commission, principle or compensating errors as these don't affect trial balance
examinerTips: Suspense accounts appear regularly in Paper 2 Section A - always check if trial balance totals disagree
When correcting multiple errors, prepare separate journal entries for each error with clear narrations
Always state whether errors affect profit calculation - this often earns easy marks
Show suspense account closing to zero after all corrections - this proves your working is correct
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Suspense Account T-format showing opening balance, corrections on appropriate sides, and closing balance of zero after all errors corrected
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (8 marks) — they asked students to prepare journal entries to correct errors found during the preparation of a trial balance and show the suspense account

O/N 2022 P2 Q3 (6 marks) tested identification of different types of errors and their effect on the trial balance, requiring students to distinguish between errors that affect trial balance totals and those that don't
M/J 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) was a multiple choice question asking which error would NOT cause a trial balance to disagree
O/N 2023 P2 Q5 (10 marks) provided a comprehensive scenario with multiple errors requiring students to prepare correcting entries, show suspense account, and prepare corrected trial balance

workedExample.question: The trial balance of ABC Trading did not agree. The difference of $420 (debit side being larger) was placed in a suspense account. The following errors were later discovered: (i) A sale of $150 was recorded in the purchases account (ii) Rent paid $80 was not recorded in the books (iii) A payment to supplier $190 was recorded as $910 in the cash book. Prepare journal entries to correct these errors and show the suspense account. (8 marks)
steps: Step 1: Analyze each error and determine if it affects the suspense account
Step 2: Error (i) - Sales $150 recorded as purchases affects both sides equally, no suspense impact
Step 3: Error (ii) - Rent $80 not recorded means both sides understated equally, no suspense impact
Step 4: Error (iii) - Cash overstated by $720 ($910-$190), this affects suspense account
Step 5: Prepare correcting journal entries for each error
Step 6: Show suspense account with opening balance and corrections
workedExample.answer:
Journal Entries:
(i) Dr Sales $150, Cr Purchases $150
(ii) Dr Rent $80, Cr Cash $80
(iii) Dr Suspense $720, Cr Cash $720

Suspense Account:
Dr side: Balance b/d $420, Cash correction $720
Cr side: Trial balance correction $1,140
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for each correct journal entry (6 marks), 2 marks for properly balanced suspense account showing the difference is cleared

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to distinguish between errors that affect the trial balance totals and those that don't' — always identify whether each error creates a suspense account entry before making corrections
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often confused the direction of suspense account entries' — remember that if debit side of trial balance is larger, the suspense account starts with a credit balance
Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates frequently omitted to show that the suspense account balance clears to zero' — always verify your suspense account balances after all corrections are made
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a T-account format for the Suspense Account showing: (1) Opening balance on the appropriate side based on trial balance difference, (2) All corrections that affect the suspense account with proper debit/credit entries, (3) Final balance should be zero when all errors are corrected, (4) Clear labeling of each entry with narrations explaining the correction being made.
note_data.quickRecap: Suspense accounts temporarily hold trial balance differences until errors are found and corrected. Only errors affecting one side of the trial balance create suspense entries. Compensating errors, errors of omission affecting both sides equally, and errors of commission don't affect suspense accounts. Always ensure the suspense account clears to zero after all corrections.
